narration: Science has a clearer story about pulsars than social media does.
visual_description: Hook visual: Science has a clearer story about pulsars than social media does.
ai_image_prompt: Hook visual: Science has a clearer story about pulsars than social media does.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Hook visual: Science has a clearer story about pulsars than social media does.

narration: Pulsars are highly magnetized, rotating neutron stars, emitting beams of radiation.
visual_description: Context visual: Pulsars are highly magnetized, rotating neutron stars, emitting beams of radiation.
ai_image_prompt: Context visual: Pulsars are highly magnetized, rotating neutron stars, emitting beams of radiation.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Context visual: Pulsars are highly magnetized, rotating neutron stars, emitting beams of radiation.

narration: They spin rapidly—up to 700 times a second—resulting in a lighthouse effect.
visual_description: Escalation visual: They spin rapidly—up to 700 times a second—resulting in a lighthouse effect.
ai_image_prompt: Escalation visual: They spin rapidly—up to 700 times a second—resulting in a lighthouse effect.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Escalation visual: They spin rapidly—up to 700 times a second—resulting in a lighthouse effect.

narration: Discovered in 1967, these cosmic clocks help measure time and test gravity.
visual_description: Evidence visual: Discovered in 1967, these cosmic clocks help measure time and test gravity.
ai_image_prompt: Evidence visual: Discovered in 1967, these cosmic clocks help measure time and test gravity.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Evidence visual: Discovered in 1967, these cosmic clocks help measure time and test gravity.

narration: But uncertainty remains—what happens when pulsars die?
visual_description: Payoff visual: But uncertainty remains—what happens when pulsars die?
ai_image_prompt: Payoff visual: But uncertainty remains—what happens when pulsars die?
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Payoff visual: But uncertainty remains—what happens when pulsars die?
